reasoning: The border was closed on May 7, 2025, over dual concerns: security (a new militant group "Mahmuda" and arms smuggling) and economic protectionism (stopping the export of agricultural commodities to Nigeria). Historically, West African border closures initiated at the presidential level take many months or even years to resolve, as seen with Nigeria's 2019 border closures. Resolving complex security issues involving militant groups and smuggling networks alongside economic policies typically requires high-level diplomatic and security negotiations. With the knowledge cutoff on May 31, 2025, there is only one month remaining until the July 1 resolution date. There have been no reported active bilateral negotiations or pending agreements that indicate an imminent reopening. Given the very short timeframe of ~30 days left before resolution, the probability of a swift reversal is quite low.
batch_queries: Will Benin re-open its closed land border crossings with Nigeria's Kwara state before July 1, 2025?
